text:
There was a wise old owl perching near the lake. Each day, he'd listen intently to the songs of the birds. One morning, a tiny hummingbird fluttered into his tree. "Ah," the owl said softly, "what a beautiful songster."

He befriended the little bird. Every afternoon, the hummingbird would visit. The owl taught it to sing a song he'd made up every afternoon.

One beautiful summer day, they flew to a field. All around, flowers swirled in the sunshine. The hummingbird sang its song.

"The sun laughs," the owl whispered.

They stayed until late. At last the owl winked.

"The moon dances," he said. Off they went.

Those are the songs I'll never forget.

Every bird has its own kind of wisdom.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
